findings.evidence: `FiniteCorestrict.lean:108` proves `corestrict_map_toLinearMap`, `FiniteCorestrict.lean:117` proves `corestrict_map_apply`, while `FiniteCorestrict.lean:166` and `:176` only compare the same unchanged underlying maps/functions after composing coalgebra morphisms.
findings.fix: Replace these with a functor-level composition statement, e.g. an equality or natural isomorphism between `corestrict (g.comp f)` and `corestrict f ⋙ corestrict g`, and derive any pointwise simp lemmas from it if still needed.
findings.issue: `corestrict_map_comp_coalg_toLinearMap` and `corestrict_map_comp_coalg_apply` are only pointwise morphism facts and are immediate special cases of `corestrict_map_toLinearMap` / `corestrict_map_apply`, so they do not express composition of corestriction at the natural categorical level.

summary: The finite corestriction functor is at the right object level, but the composition API is under-generalized and duplicates existing pointwise lemmas instead of stating the natural functor-level compatibility.
